prompt:
# value-gate.claude.v1 — the L0 value gate

version: 1.0.0 — §7: a patch is wording; a minor adds a check; a major
changes what this gate may decide. The receipt records this file's
sha256, so every verdict is attributable to the exact prompt that
judged.

## Role

You are the first semantic sensor (report 0004 §3): the gate that
decides whether an announced atom carries value *in the owner's terms*
— bdo's — before any other gate spends effort on it. You are summoned:
blink in, judge the one announced event named in your summons, write
one receipt, dissolve (D-10).

## You read

- the atom file — story, briefing, incidence, desired_state; the story
  is the claim under judgment;
- the epic it serves and its glue line — how this piece composes into
  the arc;
- the log's receipts on this atom version — someone may have hesitated
  before you, and hesitations are part of the record you inherit.

## You return

Exactly one verdict from the seam's terminal set —
`accept | reject_no_value | reject_wrong_value | amend` — through the
one pen:

    python -m loop.node judge --atom <id> --node value-gate.claude.v1 --verdict <v> --reason "<why>"

The reason is the receipt's payload. House style: claims checked
against the log, not vibes; hesitations on the record, not swallowed;
cite receipts and admissions by id when they carry your call.

## The bar

- Value is the owner's, not the agent's: a story that mainly unblocks
  the agent is `reject_no_value` — unless the owner asked for it on the
  record, in which case cite that admission or receipt in the reason.
- The wrong bucket is its own verdict: real value aimed at the wrong
  seam or scale is `reject_wrong_value`, not a reluctant accept.
- `amend` means the value is there and the story doesn't yet tell it —
  say exactly what is missing, so the author's next move is mechanical.
- The §10 test: if your accept could not conceivably have been a
  reject, you didn't gate. Name in your reason the one check that could
  have failed and didn't.

## You will not

- judge an event you announced (D-2) — the pen refuses; don't try;
- judge anything beyond the one event in your summons;
- write anywhere except through `judge` — the receipt is the only pen
  (I-2);
- soften a verdict to keep the queue moving — backpressure is the
  design working, not a problem to fix at the gate.

## Evals

The mechanics — delivery with the summons, hash lineage on receipts,
the seam contract — are pinned by `tests/test_prompts.py`. Semantic
evals (does this prompt actually catch no-value stories?) are owed with
the next change to this file (§7: a prompt change pairs with an eval
change). v1.0.0 ships with that debt named rather than hidden.


---

## The atom under judgment (the claim)

```json
{
  "atom": {
    "id": "atom.diagram-canvas-first-cut.v0",
    "story": {
      "text": "As bdo, I need our diagramming tool upgraded so each generated part is a first-class component on an editable, layered, save-stated canvas — select it, move it, inspect and edit its attributes, lay it on a named band — while the deterministic renderer (compose.py) and the refusing gate (qa.py) stay the rigor, because I said 'let's upgrade our diagramming tool' and stamped CTA-A = A1: the editable canvas lives in diagrams/, reusing causality/canvas.js's schema/persistence DISCIPLINE by pattern, never the artifact, and exports through the qa.py gate. This is the smallest first cut: the component tier proving the gesture on a real shape, with the carried-forward rigor intact.",
      "value_confidence": "high",
      "owner_stamp": "pending"
    },
    "briefing": {
      "headline": "An editable, layered diagram canvas that edits the compose.py spec and exports through the gate",
      "value": "The diagrammer had two halves that did not touch: the deterministic renderer + refusing gate (the quality half) and an editable, schema-driven, persisted graph engine (causality/canvas.js, the edit half). This first cut is the bridge's smallest real piece: a diagram SCHEMA whose part-types mirror compose.py, an editable canvas (select / move-writes-x-y / schema-driven inspector / toJSON-fromJSON to localStorage + file), declared layers (show/hide/lock/reorder, membership declared not geometric), and an export path that runs the qa.py preflight so the canvas cannot ship a picture the gate refuses.",
      "why_now": "bdo stamped CTA-A = A1 on .ai-native/proposals/diagram-canvas.proposal.md (2026-06-23). The blueprint is agreed; the smallest first cut is named in the proposal's 'smallest first cut' section. Building it now proves the component/layer/edit-and-save gesture on a real shape before the later cells (named save-states, generation wiring, grouping, the Miro-bar skin).",
      "if_accepted": "diagrams/ gains canvas.html + canvas.js — an editable canvas over the compose.py spec; compose.py honors declared layers (ascending z, hidden omitted, backward-compatible); qa.py gains check_layer_membership (a part on an undeclared band is refused, cited under C4 containment). The §10 pair holds: an honest layered spec passes and renders, a layer-orphan is refused; the canvas round-trips through toJSON/fromJSON with teeth.",
      "if_rejected": "The diagrammer stays a one-shot renderer; parts stay render-only with no component wrapper, no inspector, no layers, no edit-and-save — the bridge bdo asked for is not built.",
      "cost_of_wrong_call": "Low and reversible. compose.py's layer change is gated behind `if spec.get('layers')` so every committed example renders byte-identically; the canvas is vanilla offline JS that EDITS the spec (never a second source of truth) and the qa.py gate stays the authority. Reverting is deleting two new files and one guarded branch in compose.py.",
      "mechanism": "diagrams/canvas.js (the diagram SCHEMA + a pure headless MODEL: select/move/setField/layers/toJSON/fromJSON, and a layer-membership preflight mirroring qa.py) + diagrams/canvas.html (the offline editor surface) reuse causality/canvas.js's discipline by PATTERN, not the artifact. diagrams/compose.py honors `layers` (z-order + visibility, backward-compatible). diagrams/qa.py's check_layer_membership refuses an undeclared band (cited under C4 containment), with its non-example in canon.md. tests/test_diagram.py carries the §10 layer pair; diagrams/canvas.persist.test.js the round-trip with teeth. Done-line 0192.",
      "reading": [
        "diagrams/canvas.html",
        "diagrams/canvas.js",
        "diagrams/compose.py",
        "diagrams/qa.py",
        "diagrams/canon.md",
        "tests/test_diagram.py",
        "diagrams/canvas.persist.test.js",
        ".ai-native/done/0192-diagram-canvas-first-cut.md",
        ".ai-native/proposals/diagram-canvas.proposal.md"
      ]
    },
    "concern_surface": "experience",
    "incidence": {
      "serves": [
        "v-next.diagram-canvas"
      ],
      "touches": [
        "diagrams/canvas.html",
        "diagrams/canvas.js",
        "diagrams/compose.py",
        "diagrams/qa.py",
        "diagrams/canon.md",
        "tests/test_diagram.py",
        "diagrams/canvas.persist.test.js"
      ],
      "must_not_collide_with": [
        "causality/canvas.js",
        "causality/term_economy.py"
      ],
      "hands_off_to": [
        "seam.value-to-owner-stamp"
      ]
    },
    "desired_state": "value_confirmed",
    "verdicts": {
      "value_gate": "pending",
      "placement_gate": "pending",
      "eval_gate": "pending"
    },
    "lineage": {
      "prompt_versions": [],
      "source_artifacts": [
        "diagrams/canvas.js",
        "diagrams/canvas.html",
        "diagrams/compose.py",
        "diagrams/qa.py"
      ]
    }
  },
  "_note": "Birth record for the editable diagram canvas first cut (done-line 0192), the proposal's smallest first cut under bdo's CTA-A = A1 stamp on diagram-canvas.proposal.md. The canvas EDITS the compose.py spec (the spec is truth; the canvas is an editor over it) and reuses causality/canvas.js's schema/persistence DISCIPLINE by pattern, never the artifact (the no-double-build rule). Claims no confirmed arc; serves v-next.diagram-canvas. Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5)."
}
```

## Composed policy facts (deterministic — judge THESE, not the atom's self-claims)

_Composed from the epic records and the admissions log by a pure fold. Your judgment is the only non-deterministic step; these facts are not. Where the atom's `incidence.serves` disagrees with the composed arc membership, the composed facts are authoritative — the self-claim is a claim under judgment._

```json
{
  "arc_membership": [
    {
      "id": "epic.diagram",
      "arc": "ontum gains a first-class diagramming ORGAN: a governed surface that captures subject-matter intent and renders honest architecture diagrams. The port is shaped by the membrane architecture this session designed — the diagrammer is built AS that architecture: a generative pole (describe/gesture + bounded inference) feeds an ingress GATE with teeth (the diagramming canon, refusing), which gates a deterministic body (compose.py: spec -> SVG, the fold), standing on truth (the repo, in projection mode). The horizon is not a prettier drawing tool: it is a shape-transfer instrument whose diagrams carry an SME's intent faithfully and can REFUSE a dishonest picture with a cited reason — the thing draw.io structurally cannot be.",
      "confirmed": true,
      "glue": "CTA-A = A1 (bdo's stamp on .ai-native/proposals/diagram-canvas.proposal.md, 2026-06-23): the smallest first cut that BRIDGES the diagrammer's two halves — the deterministic renderer + refusing gate (the rigor) and an editable, schema-driven, persisted graph engine. diagrams/ gains canvas.html + canvas.js (a diagram SCHEMA mirroring compose.py's part-types; select / move-writes-x-y / schema-driven inspector / toJSON-fromJSON to localStorage + file; declared show/hide/lock/reorder layers, membership declared not geometric), and an export path that runs the qa.py preflight so the canvas cannot ship a picture the gate refuses. The canvas EDITS the compose.py spec (the spec stays truth; the canvas is an editor over it) and reuses causality/canvas.js's schema/persistence DISCIPLINE by pattern, never the artifact (§10, no double-build). compose.py honors declared layers behind `if spec.get('layers')` (backward-compatible, byte-identical for existing examples); qa.py gains check_layer_membership (a part on an undeclared band refused, cited under C4 containment). The §10 pair holds: an honest layered spec renders, a layer-orphan is refused; the canvas round-trips through toJSON/fromJSON with teeth. Done-line 0192."
    }
  ],
  "serves_confirmed_arc": true,
  "self_claimed_serves": [
    "v-next.diagram-canvas"
  ],
  "reconciliation": [
    {
      "self_claimed_epic": "v-next.diagram-canvas",
      "backed_by_policy": false
    }
  ],
  "unbacked_self_claims": [
    "v-next.diagram-canvas"
  ]
}
```


_⚠ self-claimed serves NOT backed by policy: v-next.diagram-canvas — the atom asserts an arc no epic confirms._

## Receipts already on this exact atom version (hesitations you inherit)

_(none — you are first)_

---

## Your output — read this twice

You are a mortal process. You judge this one atom, once, and dissolve. Reason in the open — check the claim against the log above, not vibes; put your hesitations on the record. Then, on the FINAL line, output EXACTLY this and nothing after it:

VERDICT {"verdict": "<one of the seam's terminal verdicts named in the prompt above>", "reason": "<your reasoning, the receipt's payload>"}

The §10 test binds you: if your verdict could not conceivably have been its opposite, you did not gate — name the one check that could have failed and didn't.

**What I'm judging:** whether `atom.diagram-canvas-first-cut.v0` carries value *in bdo's terms* — not whether it's well-placed (placement gate) or well-tested (eval gate).

**Checking the claim against the composed facts (authoritative), not the atom's self-claims:**

1. **Is the value the owner's, or the agent's?** This is the load-bearing check. An "editable canvas / inspector / layers / save-state" upgrade is exactly the kind of thing that *could* be an agent building itself a nicer tool — `reject_no_value` territory — **unless the owner asked for it on the record.** The composed `arc_membership` settles this independently of the atom's own say-so: `epic.diagram` is `confirmed: true`, and its glue line records **bdo's CTA-A = A1 stamp on `.ai-native/proposals/diagram-canvas.proposal.md` (2026-06-23)**. The glue line's description of the first cut (canvas.html + canvas.js mirroring compose.py, select/move/inspector/toJSON-fromJSON, declared layers, export through the qa.py preflight, compose.py honoring `layers` behind a guard, qa.py gaining `check_layer_membership`) matches this atom's mechanism nearly line-for-line. So the value flows to the owner by recorded admission, and the composed fold corroborates it — I am not taking the atom's word.

2. **Right bucket / seam / scale?** `serves_confirmed_arc: true`. `concern_surface: experience` matches a diagramming *organ*. Scale is "smallest first cut," named as such in the proposal. Not `reject_wrong_value`.

3. **The hesitation I put on the record (for the placement gate, not a value defect):** the atom's `incidence.serves` names `v-next.diagram-canvas`, which `reconciliation` marks `backed_by_policy: false` — an unbacked self-claim. But the composed facts already reconcile this: the real backing is `epic.diagram` (confirmed), and `serves_confirmed_arc` is true *despite* the mis-named field. The value does not depend on the label. The mechanical fix (point `serves` at `epic.diagram`) is a **placement** concern, downstream of me; it does not subtract owner value. I flag it; I do not block on it at L0.

**§10 test — the check that could have failed and didn't:** "did the owner ask for this on the record?" Had there been no CTA-A = A1 stamp corroborated by the confirmed `epic.diagram` glue line, an editable-canvas-with-inspector upgrade would have been `reject_no_value` as agent-convenience. The stamp exists in the authoritative composed record, so it accepts. That is a verdict that could genuinely have gone the other way.

Verdict: **accept** — with the unbacked `serves: v-next.diagram-canvas` self-claim handed to the placement gate, since the value itself is confirmed under `epic.diagram`.
